human.summary: Temporal substrates model is rate-separation: fast states relax to an attracting manifold; slow variables encode long-horizon drift and memory.
bullets: Separate fast x and slow τ dynamics
Slow manifold M captures stable regimes
Recency/causality handled as constraints on τ evolution

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Updates preserve causality constraints (no future leakage)
claims: Time is an explicit state variable
Verification reduces to manifold distance + causality checks
examples: [WAVE3] Example instances will be attached as child DTUs when needed.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Fast relaxation to M then slow drift along M when ε≪1 and M attracting.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

human.summary: Burst-Phase Intelligence: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Cicada: 17-year dormancy (Φ_low) accumulates B; adult emergence (Φ_high, 6 weeks): I_burst=mate-select+navigate. Buffer τ_low=17yr>>τ_high=0.12yr. D_burst·τ_high/Ḃ≈0.007 → fully recoverable. Measured by comparing emergence success rate vs. underground metabolic rate.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.potential: V_bpi=I_max-I(t); V_bpi=0 during burst, V_bpi=I_max during dormancy
math.implication: Burst-phase intelligence trades continuous function for peak I_max; viable only if buffer recovers fully during Φ_low: Ḃ·τ_low≥I_max·D_burst·τ_high.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

math.implication: Mesh intelligence reaches threshold when ∑w_{ij}>w*; below w* the mesh fragments. Measurable by measuring task performance vs. mean connection weight in distributed agent networks.

constraints: total entropy still increases (2nd law externally)
math.potential: V_lepm=‖x_plan‖²; V_lepm→0 iff planetary mind in equilibrium
math.implication: A low-entropy planetary mind maintains local entropy decrease (Lyapunov stable) against the universal entropy increase; viable iff energy throughput from the star exceeds planetary entropy production.

math.dynamics: vol(Ω_adj)=∑vol(Ω_k)-vol_overlap; transition between worlds via Ω_core

math.implication: Adjacent feasibility worlds share a non-empty core; isolated worlds have Ω_core=∅. Transition between worlds requires passing through Ω_core. Measurable as the parameter region satisfying all worlds simultaneously.

machine.primaryFormula: Ω_adj=∪_k Ω_k; adjacent iff ∩_k Ω_k≠∅; vol(Ω_adj)=∑vol(Ω_k)-vol(∩Ω_k)

Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Ecological niche saturation: bird species richness on Pacific islands. Small island ρ_sat=20 species/km². At ρ=20: V_sat_fw=0, new arrivals excluded (MacArthur-Wilson equilibrium). Immigration=Extinction=5 species/yr. Measured by species turnover rate vs. current vs. saturation density.

math.implication: World saturation occurs when density ρ≥ρ_sat; new entities cannot enter without displacing existing ones. V_sat_fw>0 triggers competitive exclusion. Measurable by population density vs. carrying capacity ratio.

V_abcv=0 iff suit meets full Venus requirements. Current best: V_abcv=0.15 (2hr operation limit). Measured by operational duration under Venus analog conditions.

examples: Self-repairing robot with nano-repair (R_art=2 damage units/hr) vs. wear (D_art=1.5 units/hr): Ḃ_art=0.5>0, viable. MTBF→∞. Standard robot (R_art=0): MTBF=B_art/D_art=1000/1.5=667hr. Repair-dominant design extends MTBF from 667hr to ∞ — measured by accelerated wear testing.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Repair-dominant artificial bodies maintain B_art indefinitely; bodies with D_art>R_art have finite lifespan T=B_art/V_rda. Measurable by MTBF (mean-time-between-failure) analysis.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

human.summary: Burst-Capable Artificial Bodies: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Explosive ordnance disposal robot: standby mode (R_art=10W charge, D_base=2W), burst mode (D_burst=500W for 30s). B_art needed=500×30=15,000J. Recovery time=15,000/(10-2)=1,875s≈31min. Falsified if burst repeated before recovery — battery depletion confirms formula.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Burst-capable artificial bodies trade continuous operation for peak I_max; feasible iff buffer recovery ≥ burst consumption. Measurable by comparing peak vs. sustained performance under power budget constraints.

machine.primaryFormula: I_burst_art=I_max·1_{B_art≥B_burst}; B_art recovers at Ḃ=R_art-D_base during standby

machine.primaryFormula: Ω_swap=∪_k Ω_module_k; V_msba=vol(Ω_task-Ω_active∩Ω_task)→0 with right modules

math.implication: Distributed artificial bodies gain advantage from decorrelated nodes; nodes sharing identical computation waste the distribution. Measured by task performance vs. node correlation structure.

math.potential: V_aili=max(0,D_infra-R_infra); V_aili=0 iff infrastructure viable indefinitely
math.implication: AI as infrastructure achieves L_infra→∞ iff R_infra≥D_infra; below this, L_infra=B_infra/(D_infra-R_infra). Measurable by system uptime vs. maintenance investment rate.

math.dynamics: I_notrain=κ_rule·vol(Ω_rules); rules cover Ω_task iff Ω_rules⊇Ω_task
math.potential: V_aiwt=vol(Ω_task)-vol(Ω_rules∩Ω_task); V_aiwt=0 iff rules cover all tasks
math.implication: Untrained AI achieves I_notrain via rule completeness; V_aiwt>0 means uncovered task regions. Measurable by task-coverage fraction of rule set vs. trained alternative.

math.dynamics: θ_{t+1}=Π_{Ω_C}[θ_t-α·∇_θ L(θ_t)]; gradient projected onto constraints
math.potential: V_cgad=L(θ)+λ·dist(θ,Ω_C)²; V_cgad→L* iff θ stays in Ω_C
math.implication: Constraint-guided development ensures θ stays in Ω_C during training; unconstrained gradient descent can exit Ω_C. Measured by constraint violation rate and performance gap vs. unconstrained training.

math.implication: AI probability surfaces partition design space into iso-EILI manifolds; moving toward higher EILI (more compute, better repair, larger memory) increases P(intelligence). Measurable by benchmarking AI performance vs. EILI score.
